post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 205: Shipping Convoy Mafia


Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!

  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 205
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Flavor came from benjaminsz
Jailkeeper wrote:
You are the captain of the Anchor Ship. You are supposed to hold the convoy in place should they be stranded over the water somewhere. However, given the recent infiltration of the convoy, you've been considering other uses for your massive anchor. You've worked out it can be used to trap one ship in place, preventing its crew from pulling any shenanigans at night, and protecting them from anyone else's shenanigans.

You win when all of the Mafia are dead.
Cop wrote:
You are the captain of the Radar Ship. In response to the infiltration of the convoy, your engineers have informed you that they've found a way to make the ship's massive radar array intercept minute radio signals, allowing you to listen in on any goings-on aboard any one other ship once per night and determine if that ship is one of the infiltrators.

You win when all of the Mafia are dead.
Doctor wrote:
You are the captain of the Materials Transport Ship. Onboard, you have a truly astounding supply of raw construction materials, and your crew just so happens to be trained in using them. Once per night, you may send them to reinforce the hull of another ship in the convoy to protect them from being sunk by infiltrators for that night.

You win when all of the Mafia are dead.
1 shot Bulletproof wrote:
You are the captain of the Armored Ship. Your ship's hull is specially reinforced, allowing it to survive the first attempt at sinking it.

You win when all of the Mafia are dead.
Tracker wrote:
You are the captain of the Spy Ship. Your crew has succeeded in placing location-tracking devices on almost every crew member in the convoy, and as such, once per night, you may investigate whether or not any crew members of one ship of your choice are up to any shenanigans.

You win when all of the Mafia are dead.
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are the captain of a Convoy Ship. Your ship and crew have no specializations - you're just an ordinary convoy member. As such, your power comes in your voice and your vote.

You win when all of the Mafia are dead.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are the captain of the Infiltrating Assassin Ship. As the leader of the infiltrating ships, you are determined to see the convoy destroyed. Why? Because you just really hate convoys for some reason. Or maybe you're in the pay of a rival shipping company. Wink, wink. Anyway, along with your partner you may decide on one ship to sink with cannon fire each night here. In addition, your crew are all well trained in the art of assassination. Once per night, you may choose a ship to send your assassins to. They will silently kill anyone who's up to anything, preventing the convoy from pulling any tricks on you.

You win when all of the Town are dead.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are the captain of an Infiltrating Ship. You are determined to see the convoy destroyed. Why? Because you just really hate convoys for some reason. Or maybe you're in the pay of a rival shipping company. Wink, wink. Anyway, along with your partner you may decide on one ship to sink with cannon fire each night here.

You win when all of the Town are dead.


Alive (5)
+mathjay
ARSM2019 snoozingnewt
G3don Mathletesv
mikimoto12 kevinmathz
Socoobo
kc5170


Dead(3)
ARay10 (Mafia Goon)
+sudzoof (Vanilla Townie)
+fishy15 (Mafia Roleblocker)


Events
Day 1: Aray10 modkilled
Night 1: No one died
Day 2: sudzoof was hanged.
Night 2: No one died.


PM Prods
 


Warnings
ARSM2019: editing


Some Mafia Guides
https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c488316_mafia_university


Story
It was just another day in port - or rather, another day before leaving port. Ten ships were about to leave in a convoy to some other port, for the trading of goods, the furthering of capitalism, blah blah blah. However, something unexpected happened. The night before the convoy was supposed to deport, a messenger arrived on board the convoy's flagship. The captain, fdas, was awoken to receive this message.

"Your convoy has been infiltrated."

Unfortunately, before he could ask for clarification, fdas heard the sound of a massive explosion. His engines had been decimated by some sort of unseen explosive. Then, without warning, the knife entered his back.

Of course, the convoy proceeded anyways, with only nine ships. Because deadlines must be met, profits must be made, etc. However, everyone knew from the events of the previous night that the convoy had been infiltrated. Now, the ships' crews would have to work out who among them was a traitor.


Do not post until this line is crossed out

Day 1 begins. Current time: 17:00:00 EDT 9 June 2020.

post_rendered:
hiya! welcome to the mafia forum!
i hope youre excited for your first mafia game cuz this is gonna be a BLAST
im mathjay, you can call me just jay or mj, and along with sudzoof and fishy, im one of the experienced players in this game. we’ll be helping you get started on the basic dos and donts of How To Win At This Gosh Darn Game so if you ever have any questions about anything, just ask!

first things first
important: if you are a power rule (basically, anything that’s town and isnt a vanilla townie), do NOT tell anyone unless you are about to die, or you get an interesting result of some sort. you should REALLY try to stay alive and hidden as long as possible. and in the case that you have questions about your role, you should probably ask them to the mod instead of in public. don’t be scared of fdas XD

also this uh should be obvious but we’ve uh had some
interesting
incidents concerning this in the past, so just in case
also important: if you’re a mafia, you should probably also. you know. stay hidden
cue intense coughing

ANYWAYS
LETS GET DOWN TO BUSINESS

NUMERO UNO: think for yourself
in a game of mafia, you should generally not trust what other people say
try not to blindly nod along and go like mmhmm i agree with absolutely everything you say and make sure to think by yourself through stuff, even if you end up agreeing
because you don’t know whether the person who’s saying whatever youre nodding along to is town or mafia, you don’t know whether theyre genuinely trying to help or if they have some nefarious ulterior motive
since this is a newbie game, if you ask a question, we three SEs will try to answer your question honestly to the best of our ability, even as scum
and if you dont trust one, you can always ask for a second opinion
XD

NUMBER TWO: how to make reads

alright so in mafia
it is important to be able to make reads: look at other people, look at what they do and what they say, and make an educated guess on which alignment you think they are
we’ll get into this more as the game goes on and you’ll figure out the nuances hopefully
but here’s the Very Rough Crash Course

there are a couple ways to scumread

the simplest way to think of scumreading
is basically looking at what people do
and thinking, would i do that if i were town? would i do that if i were scum?
would whatever they just said or did benefit town or scum more?
try to think in this mindset
for example, if someone, if sudzoof is trying extra hard to get fishy15 as fast as possible, you should probably consider whether sudzoof is town tunneling fishy because fishy was scummy, or whether sudzoof is scum trying to get a quick and easy hang off on fishy
and for stuff like trolling, where there’s no clear benefit for either town or scum, you could just say it’s NAI - non alignment indicative - and shouldn’t be really used to read people
this is motivation reading

the second part is
just keep a list of stuff that people of each alignment “usually” do
for example, people usually think town are active, contribute a lot, thinks carefully about reads before trying to hang anyone, etc etc etc.
and they think scum try to lurk (being active but just barely), try to get away with not posting a lot, are nervous about posting, have shaky and vague reads, etc etc etc
this is basically “meta”
and people think this because a lot of scum and town sometimes fit into these categories
of course, people play really really differently, which is why you should try to avoid generalizing like this
and scum of course try to avoid doing obviously scummy things XD
but keeping an eye out for conventionally scummy behaviors is a great start, before you dive deeper and look at them closer with other types of reads
these are basically experience reads or meta, doesnt really have a name but oh well

the other part is just
looking at someone’s posts
and just thinking about how they make you feel
when you look at someone’s posts, do they feel suspicious but you’re not sure why? does it sound like they’re trying to hide something, or lie about something? do you think they were caught off guard by what someone said? why?
stuff like this can count as tonereads
reading someone based on how they “sound” to you
and this is also how a lot of reads start
but you should be careful cuz tonereads aren’t always right

the last major and imo
most tricky imo type of scumreading is
interaction reads
basically, let’s say sudzoof was hanged, and he turned out to be scum
what you would do in this case
would be look at the previous day. and the wagon carefully.
who looked like they didn’t want to hang sudzoof?
who was being friendly with sudzoof?
who did sudzoof townread or scumread, and why?
is there anything irregular or suspicious or something that shouldn’t have happened?
yea
it is actually an op way of scumreading, but imo it’s pretty hard, and it doesn’t really work until you get one scum

so these are the four main types
most people mix and match, or have their favorite types
and they all work differently and have their own uses
there’s no “best” way
except keep in mind their respective weaknesses and maybe doublecheck once in a while
because...

number THREEEEE: don’t tunnel!

this happens
A LOT
basically someone goes after someone else
and theyre so so so SO convinced that theyre scum
that they literally refuse to talk about anything else
at this point you’re probably tunneling, which is bad cuz that means that you’re not considering any other possibilities
if you’re right, that’s great
but what if you’re wrong?
yea so remember to take a step back every once in a while and reevaluate
cuz we need to be smart and make sure we’re not missing anything important
and sometimes tunneling can distract us from that
also if you’re tunneling, you’re probably confirmation biased, which basically means you’re starting with the assumption that someone is scum and finding stuff to “prove it”
this doesnt really work, and it’s a trap a lot of people fall into
so it’s a nice thing to keep in mind

alright crash course over for now, sorry it was so long

hiya! welcome to the mafia forum! 
i hope youre excited for your first mafia game cuz this is gonna be a BLAST
im mathjay, you can call me just jay or mj, and along with sudzoof and fishy, im one of the experienced players in this game. we’ll be helping you get started on the basic dos and donts of How To Win At This Gosh Darn Game$^\text{TM}$ so if you ever have any questions about anything, just ask!

first things first
[b]important: if you are a power rule (basically, anything that’s town and isnt a vanilla townie), do NOT tell anyone unless you are about to die, or you get an interesting result of some sort. you should REALLY try to stay alive and hidden as long as possible. and in the case that you have questions about your role, you should probably ask them to the mod instead of in public. don’t be scared of fdas XD[/b]

also this uh should be obvious but we’ve uh had some

cue intense coughing

[b]NUMERO UNO: think for yourself[/b]
in a game of mafia, you should generally not trust what other people say
try not to blindly nod along and go like mmhmm i agree with absolutely everything you say and make sure to think by yourself through stuff, even if you end up agreeing
because you don’t know whether the person who’s saying whatever youre nodding along to is town or mafia, you don’t know whether theyre genuinely trying to help or if they have some nefarious ulterior motive
since this is a newbie game, if you ask a question, we three SEs will try to answer your question honestly to the best of our ability, even as scum
and if you dont trust one, you can always ask for a second opinion
XD

[b]NUMBER TWO: how to make reads[/b]

alright so in mafia
it is important to be able to make reads: look at other people, look at what they do and what they say, and make an educated guess on which alignment you think they are
we’ll get into this more as the game goes on and you’ll figure out the nuances hopefully
but here’s the Very Rough Crash Course$^\text{TM}$

there are a couple ways to scumread

the simplest way to think of scumreading
is basically looking at what people do
and thinking, would i do that if i were town? would i do that if i were scum? 
would whatever they just said or did benefit town or scum more? 
try to think in this mindset
for example, if someone, if sudzoof is trying extra hard to get fishy15 as fast as possible, you should probably consider whether sudzoof is town tunneling fishy because fishy was scummy, or whether sudzoof is scum trying to get a quick and easy hang off on fishy
and for stuff like trolling, where there’s no clear benefit for either town or scum, you could just say it’s NAI - non alignment indicative - and shouldn’t be really used to read people
this is motivation reading

the second part is
just keep a list of stuff that people of each alignment “usually” do
for example, people usually think town are active, contribute a lot, thinks carefully about reads before trying to hang anyone, etc etc etc. 
and they think scum try to lurk (being active but just barely), try to get away with not posting a lot, are nervous about posting, have shaky and vague reads, etc etc etc
this is basically “meta”
and people think this because a lot of scum and town sometimes fit into these categories
of course, people play really really differently, which is why you should try to avoid generalizing like this
and scum of course try to avoid doing obviously scummy things XD
but keeping an eye out for conventionally scummy behaviors is a great start, before you dive deeper and look at them closer with other types of reads
these are basically experience reads or meta, doesnt really have a name but oh well

the other part is just
looking at someone’s posts
and just thinking about how they make you feel
when you look at someone’s posts, do they feel suspicious but you’re not sure why? does it sound like they’re trying to hide something, or lie about something? do you think they were caught off guard by what someone said? why? 
stuff like this can count as tonereads
reading someone based on how they “sound” to you
and this is also how a lot of reads start
but you should be careful cuz tonereads aren’t always right

the last major and imo
most tricky imo type of scumreading is
interaction reads
basically, let’s say sudzoof was hanged, and he turned out to be scum
what you would do in this case
would be look at the previous day. and the wagon carefully. 
who looked like they didn’t want to hang sudzoof? 
who was being friendly with sudzoof? 
who did sudzoof townread or scumread, and why?
is there anything irregular or suspicious or something that shouldn’t have happened? 
yea
it is actually an op way of scumreading, but imo it’s pretty hard, and it doesn’t really work until you get one scum

so these are the four main types
most people mix and match, or have their favorite types
and they all work differently and have their own uses
there’s no “best” way
except keep in mind their respective weaknesses and maybe doublecheck once in a while
because...

[b]number THREEEEE: don’t tunnel![/b]

this happens
A LOT
basically someone goes after someone else
and theyre so so so SO convinced that theyre scum
that they literally refuse to talk about anything else
at this point you’re probably tunneling, which is bad cuz that means that you’re not considering any other possibilities
if you’re right, that’s great
but what if you’re wrong? 
yea so remember to take a step back every once in a while and reevaluate
cuz we need to be smart and make sure we’re not missing anything important

also if you’re tunneling, you’re probably confirmation biased, which basically means you’re starting with the assumption that someone is scum and finding stuff to “prove it”
this doesnt really work, and it’s a trap a lot of people fall into

ARSM2019 wrote:
can i refrain from voting today? perhaps we will have more evidence on the second day

you can say vote: no hang but put it differently(I did that on purpose)
okay so @both of you, you basically never no hang as town
the basic principle is that
any hang is better than a night kill
because a hang has at least a small chance of hitting scum even if random
while a night kill has a 0% chance of ever hitting scum
and since a no hang is basically giving scum an extra night kill
we basically never nightkill unless there is a big mechanical reason
i can explain more if you want but it's rarely ever used

so you dont have to come up with a hang right this instant
but most cases its better to try to go with your best guess, rather than nothing

I agree with you that defensiveness is iffy. In my opinion, a moderate amount of defensiveness might be a townread but being over-defensive is pretty scummy, and I do think that typing like this asking people to unvote you is a bit over-defensive.

As for the V/LA read, sorry, I didn’t know that it was frowned upon to read on V/LAs. Is it considered OOT?

Anyway, my point about the panicking is that town members usually don’t panic when they are being accused of scummy actions. Why? Because they have nothing to worry about. They have a solid defense because they are town. Even if they do get hanged and they flip green, it gives the town information that the remaining players can then use to figure out who is mafia and who is town. Mafia tend to panic when they are accused of being scummy. However, I will say that panicking and “not wanting to die” could be attributed to inexperience. Newbies tend to try as hard as possible to stay alive until the end of the game for some reason even though that is not exactly the point of the game. You can still win when you’re dead, and that is a fact that people who are new to the game tend to ignore.
fishy15 wrote:
@scoobo i feel like a lot of your post is influenced by conf bias
since you think miki is scum, you say that everything they do is scummy when i can see the exact same thing happening from a town perspective tbh

Okay, I admit that some of the things he did could possibly be attributed to being inexperienced, but I did not account for this when I was writing up some of the other posts since he seemed to be using mafia terms quite fluently. But I cannot see the “exact same thing happening” if he was a non-gamethrowing town who was reasonably experienced (if he was not a newbie).

I’m not pulling reads out of nowhere. These are established things that you have to look for when you are scumreading.

Contradictory Statements - Goes on V/LA for being busy with schoolwork then saying he has nothing to do. (I know mathjay said reading based on V/LAs are frowned upon. I didn’t know this, sorry ) This would be like claiming cop on day 3 then claiming BP on day 4. It makes no sense for a town member to do this.

Random Bandwagon - Correct me I am wrong but mathjay was being sarcastic/joking when in his post when he said “imagine just making reads.” And then mikimoto12 demands an explanation of what “just making reads” means because he apparently “agrees with above.” Analogously, imagine if someone was confirmed as an important town role. Call this guy A. If B then sarcastically/jokingly goes “hey A is coasting let’s hang him” and C then randomly bandwagons and goes “A, role?” even though he is already confirmed, what would you think about C? Would he seem like town to you?

All Caps - It is a pretty well known pattern that if someone types in ALL CAPS FOR A WHOLE SENTENCE, or in this case if he’s typing like this then they tend to be scum more often than not.

What I’m trying to say is that the reads can hardly be considered as confirmation bias. These are patterns that people look for when scumreading. Although I will repeat that I think some of the things he did can be attributed to inexperience.

so thats why people want to stay alive

That is strictly not true. Your wincon as town is to kill all mafia, not stay alive at the end of the game. I’ve been in numerous mafia games where the death of town(s) (either VT or PR, and either by hang or by mafia night kill) allowed town to win by giving enough information for town to push the remaining mafia members. Such scenarios are not uncommon. As an SE, I’m sure you have been in these situations at least once or twice.

As a very clear example, in mafia.gg there is a role called Lamb, whose job is literally to commit suicide in exchange for revealing someone else’s role. Dying as town does not necessarily go against wincon. It can clear up a lot of doubts on whose role is what and take away some claim space from scum.

Experienced, high-skill players do not panic when accused, even when they are mafia. They will simply give a logical argument, a good claim, give their notes/will (different names for the same thing, in ToS it’s called a will) and hope for the best. In any case, panicking does nothing to help your case; quite to the contrary, it will make people assume that you are mafia. Though it does take quite a few games of mafia to develop this skill of not panicking on the stand (I guess it’s a skill? Maybe a personality trait?).

post_rendered:
I haven't finished yet, but I have a few impressions:

mathjay -> The excitement in his first post seems like an interpersonal characteristic of him, possibly demonstrated in other games. Although the post was useful, in a way, I feel that it is essentially IIoA (information instead of analysis) and is not a criterion for assessing alignment. Post 3 is an extension of that. Posts 16, 19, 30 are neutral interactions. Post 61 is instructive and seems transparent in the sense of genuineness. 66 is a brief interaction that maintains the tone of ''guide''.

Conclusion: The instructive tone and the transparent articulation displayed between his posts seem to me to be positive traits of an attempt to integrate with the players, mainly by sharing knowledge. I do not rule out, however, the hypothesis that this player is camouflaging himself under the harmless pretext of ''helping the newbies'', but at the moment I feel comfortable about him.

sudzoof -> In his first post there was a deviation from the RQS, accompanied by a small expansion of instructional advice and a brief questioning of a topic that Mathjay had commented on, but nothing so expressive in terms of alignment. Although the intention was to expand the advice for newbies, I feel that the tone was more apathetic compared to Mathjay. The vote on post 12 was probably a suggestive reaction to the response Mathjay would eventually give on post 16. Post 23 is a neutral interaction. The vote on post 37 had a deliberate misspelling to test Mikimoto's reaction, something that seemed a little too obvious and may have been comically motivated to promote interaction. Post 60 he clarifies that the vote was false. Post 62 he votes again, this time correctly, probably influenced by the sporadic posture of kc5170 between posts 47 and 48.

Conclusion: The tone does not give me much confidence, but I feel that objectivity and a less energetic posture compared to Mathjay may be an alternative way of approaching the game, which makes me curious to see how he will play from this point on.

ARSM2019 -> His first post skipped the RQS, but it still looks like a normal introduction. On post 43, he asks something that sounds strange to me, because in the previous post he said he was new to this mafia format, but not to the mafia game itself, which makes me think that he should already know this type of information, assuming that he probably played the theme before.

Conclusion: Although his content is basically shallow comments, I have a bad impression of the player due to the strange interpretation that the statement on post 36 and the question on post 43 reflected in my perspective.

post_rendered:
First off, a friendly PSA for people who haven't confirmed to confirm ASAP. Also, I'm gonna Unvote because mikimoto12 seems to me more like inexperienced town than mafia (no offense).
sudzoof wrote:
well yes, but from anyone's PoV, they're conftown, and hanging a conftown usually doesn't help with hanging scum.

Well, you see, the point of Mafia is not how you view yourself but how others view you. I don't think anyone here is naive enough to think that "just because I know I'm confirmed town means that everyone else does as well."

Also, try not to think of town deaths as absolutely bad. Too many town is just as bad as too few, especially if we consider many VTs. The reason is simple: too much claimspace for mafia may result in accidentally "confirming" a mafia member as town. This is extremely harmful for town as when someone is "confirmed" it usually takes a great deal of effort to "un-confirm" them. Furthermore, another downside to having too many town is that a series of mislynches and night kills may quickly cut the town majority down to a losing position, and this is especially true in setups where mafia or neutrals have high kill potential. You would be hard-pressed to find a town victory in which mafia did not gamethrow and no townies died by the end of the game. (I still haven't earned that achievement for perfect town win in Town of Salem in 5 years of playing that game.)

In setups like these where it's 7/2/0, town wants to shoot for anywhere between a 3 for 2 trade or a 2 for 1 trade. When townies die, it takes away claimspace from mafia and narrows down the list of suspects. In addition, we can see who was pushing the townie and use this to possibly scumread the mafia.

Consider an alternate version of Mafia. In this game, we have 1 continuous day phase and that's it. It's a 9/1/0 setup, and you have 3 weeks in real life to discuss and hang someone. If the person you hang is the mafia, town wins. If the person you hang is town, town loses. Mafia would win 99% of the time due to the simple fact that there is too many town.

G3don's Analysis
G3don wrote:
I haven't finished yet, but I have a few impressions:

mathjay -> The excitement in his first post seems like an interpersonal characteristic of him, possibly demonstrated in other games. Although the post was useful, in a way, I feel that it is essentially IIoA (information instead of analysis) and is not a criterion for assessing alignment. Post 3 is an extension of that. Posts 16, 19, 30 are neutral interactions. Post 61 is instructive and seems transparent in the sense of genuineness. 66 is a brief interaction that maintains the tone of ''guide''.

Conclusion: The instructive tone and the transparent articulation displayed between his posts seem to me to be positive traits of an attempt to integrate with the players, mainly by sharing knowledge. I do not rule out, however, the hypothesis that this player is camouflaging himself under the harmless pretext of ''helping the newbies'', but at the moment I feel comfortable about him.

sudzoof -> In his first post there was a deviation from the RQS, accompanied by a small expansion of instructional advice and a brief questioning of a topic that Mathjay had commented on, but nothing so expressive in terms of alignment. Although the intention was to expand the advice for newbies, I feel that the tone was more apathetic compared to Mathjay. The vote on post 12 was probably a suggestive reaction to the response Mathjay would eventually give on post 16. Post 23 is a neutral interaction. The vote on post 37 had a deliberate misspelling to test Mikimoto's reaction, something that seemed a little too obvious and may have been comically motivated to promote interaction. Post 60 he clarifies that the vote was false. Post 62 he votes again, this time correctly, probably influenced by the sporadic posture of kc5170 between posts 47 and 48.

Conclusion: The tone does not give me much confidence, but I feel that objectivity and a less energetic posture compared to Mathjay may be an alternative way of approaching the game, which makes me curious to see how he will play from this point on.

ARSM2019 -> His first post skipped the RQS, but it still looks like a normal introduction. On post 43, he asks something that sounds strange to me, because in the previous post he said he was new to this mafia format, but not to the mafia game itself, which makes me think that he should already know this type of information, assuming that he probably played the theme before.

Conclusion: Although his content is basically shallow comments, I have a bad impression of the player due to the strange interpretation that the statement on post 36 and the question on post 43 reflected in my perspective.


This was a pretty long quote so I used hide
 
. Just as a friendly reminder, I think the rules said that you should avoid colors like green and red as much as possible because it's what the mods use for announcements and stuff.

The way I see it, there are two parts to the post. The first part of the post uses tone and style of writing to establish a townread on mathjay and sudzoof. The second part uses post 43 as evidence against ARSM2019.

As for the first part, this isn’t exactly how tonereading works.

First of all, tonereading doesn’t go both ways. A panicked, emotional, overdefensive, and hasty tone may indicate scum but a calm, logical, objective, and confident tone does not necessarily indicate town. As I have mentioned before, experienced and high-skilled players will almost never lose their composure even when they are mafia. This, added to the fact that you are trying to toneread the SEs, who are experienced and high-skill players, makes me doubt the accuracy of your logic.

Second of all, you can only toneread when someone gets accused of being mafia. Anyone can have a confident, transparent, “guiding” tone when they have got nothing to lose, even a noob can do this. As you said, mathjay’s post was IIoA and that should be the end of it. Having a “good” tone is easy when you’re not being accused, and neither mathjay nor sudzoof have been accused of anything.

Now, as for your suspicion on ARSM2019, I would argue that this suspicion is unfounded unless mikimoto12 is indeed mafia. If you take in the context that I was accusing mikimoto12 of being mafia at the time, post 43 seems like an attempt to distract from this conversation and protect a fellow mafia member from being hanged on day 1. However, I currently have the impression that mikimoto12 is an inexperienced town rather than mafia. There is nothing wrong about asking for a no hang as many games like mafia.gg and Town of Salem favor no hangs on day 1.

Well, you see, the point of Mafia is not how you view yourself but how others view you. I don't think anyone here is naive enough to think that "just because I know I'm confirmed town means that everyone else does as well."

Also, try not to think of town deaths as absolutely bad. Too many town is just as bad as too few, especially if we consider many VTs. The reason is simple: too much claimspace for mafia may result in accidentally "confirming" a mafia member as town. This is extremely harmful for town as when someone is "confirmed" it usually takes a great deal of effort to "un-confirm" them. Furthermore, another downside to having too many town is that a series of mislynches and night kills may quickly cut the town majority down to a losing position, and this is especially true in setups where mafia or neutrals have high kill potential. You would be hard-pressed to find a town victory in which mafia did not gamethrow and no townies died by the end of the game. (I still haven't earned that achievement for perfect town win in Town of Salem in 5 years of playing that game.)

post_rendered:
I returned.

In response to post 101 (Mathjay): yes, I had the conclusion that he would be town due to the contrast I mentioned on post 96, between the interaction with me and his behavior with others. My impression of the most beneficial action taken by mafia comes from a logical point of view, as I don't see how attracting attention would be positive for an alignment that, as a rule, needs to captivate collective trust to extend its survival in the game. The point you commented about the contrary situation occurring on purpose seems plausible, but not as likely in a game made up of a newbie base, as this would imply that mobsters with little affinity with the mechanics of the game and the website would be able to use this psychological device to gain an advantage, when in fact, they would be more susceptible to errors by the stress that this move would require to be convincing. In the balance of probabilities, I prefer to be optimistic and by the concept of occam's razor to infer that Socoobo is town.

I believe I already answered the question of posts 103/107 (Mathjay) earlier on my post 100 - But, in summary, aggressiveness is a suspect element when applied in isolation, without a larger context. If Socoobo had a neutral approach to the game, but interacted specifically with me aggressively, there could be a tendency behind this, because, in theory, it is difficult for a behavioral deviation in a standard line to be characterized involuntarily, without the user having knowledge of that. Well, I hope this made sense to you.

In response to post 102 (Socoobo): I confess that I was uncomfortable, but not to the point of being offended. I respect your choice of playstyle and I also understand that relying on reads through the observation of tonality is not exactly the best method, although it is the way I feel comfortable using. Regarding ARSM, I agree that within the set of experiences that he may have had with the topic on other platforms, the voting circumstances could be different, which would make his doubt true, but I feel that perhaps he could have asked privately to the moderator, which still makes my bad impression about him remain, although it is not enough to characterize a vote.

I am pretending to be a noob mafia in order to get ARa10 voted off. By saying things like 'ARa10 isn't mafia' I managed to make us both look suspicious (This is intentional.) Quite to the contrary, you have a high chance of being mafia if ARay10 attacks you.

We see that there are four cases for the alignments of ARay10 and ARSM2019.

The first case is v/v. I think this is unlikely, given the truckload of scummy actions from both players, but we will still analyze this. It would be absolutely implausible for ARay10 as a town member to actively attack the person who is defending him. I would actually consider this soft gamethrowing unless you were actively trying to get killed for strategic purposes, which ARay10 is clearly not. It would also be implausible for ARSM2019 to defend him in the first place, since he is a town who has absolutely no information other than scumreading, and from scumreading I can say that ARay10 is pretty suspicious. Neither side would have a reason to do what they did, so this is most likely not the case.

The second case is v/w. In this case, it would be plausible for ARSM2019 to defend ARay10, since as a mafia member, ARSM2019 knows who is town and who is mafia. It would be beneficial to ARSM2019 to have "saved" a town member from being hanged, because then ARSM2019 would gain the town's trust. But it very much implausible for ARay10 as town to attack the person who is defending him, for the same reason as before.

The third case is w/v. In this case, it would not be plausible for ARay10 to attack ARSM2019, who he knows is town. Even if he succeeds in getting a hang on ARSM2019, town will likely hang him the next day without much opposition, making it at worst a 2 for 1 trade leaving the town as 5/1/0, which is favorable for town in a 7/2/0 setup. It would also not be plausible for ARSM2019 to defend ARay10 for the same reason as the first case.

Finally, we have the w/w case. In this case, it would be plausible for ARSM2019 to defend ARay10, since he is a fellow mafia member. It would also make sense if ARay10 attacked ARSM2019, because he knows that he will most likely be hanged and he wants to make town think "Oh, mafia attacked ARSM2019, he must be town!" This I feel is the most likely case. If mathjay is indeed correct that mafia does not have daychat in this game, then this could possibly be the reason that the two mafia members weren't really on the same page.

Even if he succeeds in getting a hang on ARSM2019, town will likely hang him the next day without much opposition, making it at worst a 2 for 1 trade leaving the town as 5/1/0, which is favorable for town in a 7/2/0 setup. I simply said that you should pause your scumhunting if you are the one being scumhunted, not that you shouldn't scumhunt altogether.

When you get called out for saying/doing things that seem suspicious, you should explain what happened/why you did it. That is what I consider a defense, and it does not include attacking someone else. You do not have to attack someone else to defend yourself. Right now, the two possible setups are:

5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

Mafia knows which setup we have. If they have a roleblocker, we have the first. Otherwise, if both are mafia goons, we have the second.

Now, if we consider the first setup, then one of the following things happened last night.

1. Jailkeeper jailed mafia.
2. Mafia shot the 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie (kc5170).
3. Mafia did not attack.

Option 3 is quite implausible since option 2 is strictly better, so we can narrow down the choices to the top 2 options. The optimal play for mafia is option 2 (but they really have no choice in the matter if they get jailed).

Considering the second setup, one of two things happened last night.

1. Mafia shot the 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie (kc5170).
2. Mafia did not attack.

If tracker exists and finds a visiting role, he/she should claim immediately since the town only has one visiting role and that is the tracker themselves. Hence their target must be mafia. If sudzoof is scum, option 2 would be optimal for him since he knows there is an extremely high chance tracker will target him. Otherwise, option 1 is optimal.

I think it would be safe to assume that jailkeeper/tracker targeted sudzoof last night, so if kc5170 dies before another no-kill night, sudzoof is confirmed because night 1 would have been the night when mafia first attacked kc5170, and sudzoof could not or did not visit.

My hang priority is
1. sudzoof
2. Mathletesv

Hot takes aren't exactly my thing but here's a cold take: hang sudzoof, jail/track Mathletesv if sudzoof is town.

G3don wrote:
I haven't finished yet, but I have a few impressions:

Click to reveal hidden text
mathjay -> The excitement in his first post seems like an interpersonal characteristic of him, possibly demonstrated in other games. Although the post was useful, in a way, I feel that it is essentially IIoA (information instead of analysis) and is not a criterion for assessing alignment. Post 3 is an extension of that. Posts 16, 19, 30 are neutral interactions. Post 61 is instructive and seems transparent in the sense of genuineness. 66 is a brief interaction that maintains the tone of ''guide''.

Conclusion: The instructive tone and the transparent articulation displayed between his posts seem to me to be positive traits of an attempt to integrate with the players, mainly by sharing knowledge. I do not rule out, however, the hypothesis that this player is camouflaging himself under the harmless pretext of ''helping the newbies'', but at the moment I feel comfortable about him.

sudzoof -> In his first post there was a deviation from the RQS, accompanied by a small expansion of instructional advice and a brief questioning of a topic that Mathjay had commented on, but nothing so expressive in terms of alignment. Although the intention was to expand the advice for newbies, I feel that the tone was more apathetic compared to Mathjay. The vote on post 12 was probably a suggestive reaction to the response Mathjay would eventually give on post 16. Post 23 is a neutral interaction. The vote on post 37 had a deliberate misspelling to test Mikimoto's reaction, something that seemed a little too obvious and may have been comically motivated to promote interaction. Post 60 he clarifies that the vote was false. Post 62 he votes again, this time correctly, probably influenced by the sporadic posture of kc5170 between posts 47 and 48.

Conclusion: The tone does not give me much confidence, but I feel that objectivity and a less energetic posture compared to Mathjay may be an alternative way of approaching the game, which makes me curious to see how he will play from this point on.

ARSM2019 -> His first post skipped the RQS, but it still looks like a normal introduction. On post 43, he asks something that sounds strange to me, because in the previous post he said he was new to this mafia format, but not to the mafia game itself, which makes me think that he should already know this type of information, assuming that he probably played the theme before.

Conclusion: Although his content is basically shallow comments, I have a bad impression of the player due to the strange interpretation that the statement on post 36 and the question on post 43 reflected in my perspective.

This is an analysis on what we should do next.

NOTE: I obviously have no idea who will get killed at night so I ignore night deaths in this post.

1. Hang - sudzoof

If all goes well, this is the end of the game. Otherwise, we have 6-1-0 and we look at who pushed sudzoof most aggressively: Mathletesv.

2. Jail/Track - Mathletesv

This will pretty much disable him for the night. Obviously, if tracker finds that he is a visiting role, we hang him.

Otherwise, we have two cases.

Plan 1
If mafia gets a night kill, and Mathletesv did not visit, we can clear Mathletesv. At this point we have 5-1-0.

3. Hang - fishy15

fishy15 is an improbable choice for sure, and I don't think it will come down to this but with sudzoof dead and Mathletesv cleared, this is our third-best choice. I have not seen enough of kevinmathz to make a judgement on him but otherwise, mathjay and snoozingnewt are pretty much guaranteed town, and kc5170 is confirmed. If the game doesn't end here, we have 4-1-0.

4. Jail/Track - Socoobo

I'm not confirmed, and I think that I am definitely on top of the scummy list if sudzoof, Mathletesv, and fishy15 have all been removed. This may help clear me or it may do nothing at all if mafia does not go for a night kill.

After this, there are two options.

Plan 1.1
If I am jailed/tracked and mafia goes for the night kill, I will be cleared. Hence the hang pool will be set at {mathjay, snoozingnewt, kevinmathz}, and we have 3-1-0.

5. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

This narrows down the pool to two people. We are at 3-1-0 with at least two confirmed town. Hang one and jail/track the other gives us the win.

Plan 1.2
If I am jailed/tracked and mafia does not go for a night kill, I will not be cleared. We are still at 4-1-0.

5. Hang - Socoobo

I am just a VT so I'm not that useful anyways. We then can narrow down the pool to {mathjay, snoozingnewt, kevinmathz}. Now we are at 3-1-0.

6. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

This narrows down the pool to two people. We are at 3-1-0 with at least two confirmed town. Jail/track one and hang the other gives us the win.

Plan 2
If mafia does not get a night kill, and Mathletesv does not visit anyone, we cannot clear Mathletesv. We are still at 6-1-0.

3. Hang - Mathletesv

This is the most logical choice to me because of the things I have said in post 912. I feel like he is the second most suspicious person. After this hang, if Mathletesv is mafia, the game ends. Otherwise, we are at 5-1-0 and we look to a very improbable choice.

4. Jail/Track - fishy15

Again, fishy15 is a very unlikely candidate for mafia, but at this point we have no other choice. This can either clear fishy15 or do nothing if mafia does not go for a night kill.

We then have two options.

Plan 2.1
If mafia gets a night kill, and fishy15 was jailed/tracked, we can clear fishy15. This leaves us at 4-1-0.

5. Hang - Socoobo

I'm a VT and I'm not very useful so it's not a big loss anyways. We narrow the hang pool with this and we are left at 3-1-0.

6. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

This narrows down the hang pool to two people. 3-1-0 with two confirmed town again, which is a win after we hang one and jail/track the other.

Plan 2.2
If mafia does not have a night kill, we cannot clear fishy15. This leaves us still at 5-1-0.

5. Hang - fishy15

Again, fishy15 is our third-best choice so, if we hang him and he flips town, we are left at 4-1-0. Our hangpool is {mathjay, snoozingnewt, Socoobo, kevinmathz}.

6. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

The claim narrows down the hangpool to three people (two from my point of view, but you guys don't actually know for certain that I'm town). At this point, since mafia has not gotten night kills at all, we may assume that kc5170 has lost his bulletproof.

If you guys are willing to believe I'm town, then we hang one and jail/track the other. Otherwise, I'm pretty sure we lose this.

post_rendered:
It was a foggy night, and it was difficult for the ships to see each other. At times, the indistinct outline of a ship would fade and another would appear. When the day finally began, the fog having over the ships lifted and finally the ships could see each other once again. They began counting the ships. One. Two. Three. Four. Five. Six. Seven. There were only seven ships, out of the nine that began the journey. Though they had taken losses earlier, it seems they had made it through another night unscathed.

Day 3 begins. Current time: 21:00:00 EDT 27 July 2020.

Socoobo wrote:
This is an analysis on what we should do next.

NOTE: I obviously have no idea who will get killed at night so I ignore night deaths in this post.

1. Hang - sudzoof

If all goes well, this is the end of the game. Otherwise, we have 6-1-0 and we look at who pushed sudzoof most aggressively: Mathletesv.

2. Jail/Track - Mathletesv

This will pretty much disable him for the night. Obviously, if tracker finds that he is a visiting role, we hang him.

Otherwise, we have two cases.

Plan 1
If mafia gets a night kill, and Mathletesv did not visit, we can clear Mathletesv. At this point we have 5-1-0.

3. Hang - fishy15

fishy15 is an improbable choice for sure, and I don't think it will come down to this but with sudzoof dead and Mathletesv cleared, this is our third-best choice. I have not seen enough of kevinmathz to make a judgement on him but otherwise, mathjay and snoozingnewt are pretty much guaranteed town, and kc5170 is confirmed. If the game doesn't end here, we have 4-1-0.

4. Jail/Track - Socoobo

I'm not confirmed, and I think that I am definitely on top of the scummy list if sudzoof, Mathletesv, and fishy15 have all been removed. This may help clear me or it may do nothing at all if mafia does not go for a night kill.

After this, there are two options.

Plan 1.1
If I am jailed/tracked and mafia goes for the night kill, I will be cleared. Hence the hang pool will be set at {mathjay, snoozingnewt, kevinmathz}, and we have 3-1-0.

5. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

This narrows down the pool to two people. We are at 3-1-0 with at least two confirmed town. Hang one and jail/track the other gives us the win.

Plan 1.2
If I am jailed/tracked and mafia does not go for a night kill, I will not be cleared. We are still at 4-1-0.

5. Hang - Socoobo

I am just a VT so I'm not that useful anyways. We then can narrow down the pool to {mathjay, snoozingnewt, kevinmathz}. Now we are at 3-1-0.

6. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

This narrows down the pool to two people. We are at 3-1-0 with at least two confirmed town. Jail/track one and hang the other gives us the win.

Plan 2
If mafia does not get a night kill, and Mathletesv does not visit anyone, we cannot clear Mathletesv. We are still at 6-1-0.

3. Hang - Mathletesv

This is the most logical choice to me because of the things I have said in post 912. I feel like he is the second most suspicious person. After this hang, if Mathletesv is mafia, the game ends. Otherwise, we are at 5-1-0 and we look to a very improbable choice.

4. Jail/Track - fishy15

Again, fishy15 is a very unlikely candidate for mafia, but at this point we have no other choice. This can either clear fishy15 or do nothing if mafia does not go for a night kill.

We then have two options.

Plan 2.1
If mafia gets a night kill, and fishy15 was jailed/tracked, we can clear fishy15. This leaves us at 4-1-0.

5. Hang - Socoobo

I'm a VT and I'm not very useful so it's not a big loss anyways. We narrow the hang pool with this and we are left at 3-1-0.

6. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

This narrows down the hang pool to two people. 3-1-0 with two confirmed town again, which is a win after we hang one and jail/track the other.

Plan 2.2
If mafia does not have a night kill, we cannot clear fishy15. This leaves us still at 5-1-0.

5. Hang - fishy15

Again, fishy15 is our third-best choice so, if we hang him and he flips town, we are left at 4-1-0. Our hangpool is {mathjay, snoozingnewt, Socoobo, kevinmathz}.

6. Claim - JailKeeper/Tracker

The claim narrows down the hangpool to three people (two from my point of view, but you guys don't actually know for certain that I'm town). At this point, since mafia has not gotten night kills at all, we may assume that kc5170 has lost his bulletproof.

If you guys are willing to believe I'm town, then we hang one and jail/track the other. Otherwise, I'm pretty sure we lose this.

MVP Order

1. mathjay

Carried town to the win. What an amazing fishy15 scumread. Had a lot of really good contributions.
We would probably have taken at least 2 more days to win if it wasn't for mathjay.
Decisive PR claim was pretty clutch as well.

2. fishy15

Did a great job of concealing himself as wolf. Almost no one except the two SEs scumread him.
I kind of became suspicious of him after the sudzoof hang, but it was for the wrong reason.

3. sudzoof

Really good read on fishy15. Too bad we didn't listen to you.
Could have been a bit more active I guess.

4. snoozingnewt

Towards the end, wasn't as active, but did a good job in getting himself townread as soon as possible.
Pretty much confirmed town on everyone's radars, which meant that we had one less person to worry about.

5. Mathletesv

Overall great pushing, just slightly too impatient and spammy.
Also did some questionable (slightly scummy) things but was really decisive in pushing for the hammers.
